content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments are verified against the source code and address real defects. (1) Duration overflow panic is confirmed — trivial saturating_add fix. (2) Process kill targets only the immediate child PID with no process group setup at spawn, leaking grandchildren on timeout. (3) Snapshot remains Running during backoff sleep while resume rejects Running snapshots, creating permanent stuck state on crash.

## Votes

- `fr-13-787f9c72`: Accept
  Verified at service.rs:210 — `request.timeout + Duration::from_secs(30)` uses Duration::add which panics on overflow via checked_add().expect(). Replacing with saturating_add is a zero-risk, zero-cost defensive fix that eliminates a real panic path. Even though Duration::MAX timeouts are unlikely in practice, the fix is trivial and correct.
- `fr-13-43e2123e`: Accept
  Verified: send_signal (line 367) sends to positive PID (single process only), Command::new at line 985-992 has no setsid/setpgid/process_group configuration, and kill_on_drop(true) only kills the immediate child. Any backend that forks (shell wrappers, CLI tools spawning subprocesses) will leave grandchildren alive after timeout. This violates the timeout contract and leaks resources. The fix requires spawning in a new process group and killing the group on timeout/cancel.
- `fr-13-997cd9b5`: Accept
  Verified all three legs: (1) snapshot persisted as RunStatus::Running at line 4472, (2) after retryable failure, stage_failed is journaled and backoff sleep occurs at lines 4637-4640 (up to 60s) with no snapshot update to a resumable state, (3) resume at lines 1155-1159 explicitly rejects Running snapshots. If the process crashes during the backoff window, the project is permanently stuck — Running (so resume rejects it) but no process is active. This is a real stuck-state bug requiring either persisting a resumable state before sleeping or allowing resume to recover stale Running snapshots.
